item.title: Large Loud Husky Dump
item.description:
I love huskies. I love the husky videos. I thought I’d make a dump. MVEdit: send talking dogs & cats
I do not want a husky however.
I would not be a good husky mom.
I love how derpy they are. Edit: credit for this dreaming doggo to @theSilencewillfall
How absolutely goofballs they are.
But holy cow they are so loud.
And argumentative.
And generally too smart. For me.
My parents had a brush wolf / malamute when I was born.
Her name was Tequila.
If I had a husky I would name in Moon Moon because I still laugh at that.
And also because I haven’t put much thought into naming a husky because I do not want a husky.
But I absolutely love & enjoy
Videos of other peoples’ huskies.
So I thought
Maybe I’m not the only one.
And in these perpetually unprecedented for one reason or another times
Maybe a dump of huskies hollering, talking & being huskies
Would be enjoyed by others as well.
So here it is. I mean look at that hollering chonk.
If I stole a video from you, thank you for sharing it.
It is appreciated.
So conversational.
These videos always crack me up.
Also the ones of angry cats singing.
Maybe if I lived alone
I could devote my life to one or two
Adorable.
But not in my current life.
That energy level omg
I’ve probably spent 15 minutes of my life watching this and giggling. I have no idea why. It just makes me laugh.
See that? I couldn’t be responsible for that level of dogging.

item.title: Not a good day for my boss..
item.description: Same old same old: too much work, long hours, pay no up to par, got 5mo child at home. Decided to change scenery and profession alltogether. Been planning to quit for months but wife had no job so I couldn't leave us on nothing. Feels like I've been burning the candle from both ends, and there is not much to personal life since I feel a bit burnout creeping up. House needs fixing, kid needs attention and wife needs a husband, but after workday I'm too exhausted to do anything. Weekends go in a blur, and joy has left my life. Finally yesterday she got a job and starts in two months. Figured I could leave my letter of resignation with 2 months notice and be stay-at-home dad for a while. Broke my ankle last September so my foot could use the downtime. I printed two copies and signed them, figured I'd give em to boss after the day is done. Suddenly coworker stated at coffee break with a bang that he quits and our boss can shove it. "This is April Fool's, right?" 'NOPE, I'LL GET MY SHIT NEXT MONDAY!' Uses all his vacation days and left doors banging. Boss is furious. So much work to do, nobody to do them. The position he left is almost the same as mine. I'm bit afraid to walk in his office in 4 hours to tell him I doing the same thing. Wish me luck Imgurites! MV EDIT: It's done. Walked to bosses office and handed him the papers. "Are you fucking kidding me??". Told him that I was going to take time for myself and my family. Got offered whopping 0,6% payraise, I told him that this wasn't about the money, I could almost see the ??? above his head. "Let's talk this through Monday, aight?" Sure, I'm still employed here for two months, but my mind is made up. If I have to choose between work and family, family wins 100 times out of 100. He sighed and signed the papers. Gotta make documentation of my work for some poor bastard to take over after I'm gone. I work at multimillion process-automation company, so finding replacement for me shouldn't be too hard. Thank you all for your kind words! I haven't felt this happy in 2 years. I'll celebrate with a nice pizza pie tonight! Stay safe! EDIT2: Office Manager landed on my desk as I was packing and said "You should think before making these kind of tasteless April Fool's declarations. *Boss' name* is really furious", with a doubtful grin. I told him I was dead serious, and this was in no way a April Fool's. "Naaah, this is a joke, you're joking. We got lots of work orders to deliver". Welp, see if it's April Fool's at monday. I was thinking ordering deep dish pizza, haven't had one of those in a while. PHIIS!
item.dc:creator: Grindwinder

item.title: 20 vids and 24 memes, my most recent steals, please enjoy
item.description:
Let's go
Drunk save
Did a deep YouTube search of him and was not upset about it
Quickly
just stfu lol
I lost one recently
Ahhh
I will never stop loving this one
Incredible
I feel like this is obligatory in every video dump
She's fun
Just a masterpiece, I find myself randomly make the same sounds.
Fantastic
Idk if this is accurate but fun to watch
I can loop this for a while
Bravo
Just a smexy wave
Hopefully will manifest this, this summer
Remember, cooking is dangerous
In case u need it
End of vids, welcome to meme portion
I love fevers
Me too buddy
Age of empires meme and a hard burn too
The realist
It would
Not terrible
Saved this for the children
Saved this for my next tele health visit
Just like
Those Brits eh
In case I may need this ever
I feel this
Saving for emergencies
Looks like my ex
This must be good, not reading it again, lmk
Happens a lot
Sad feels
Another important document
loled again
What a vision
Excellent quality
Sadly yes
Just in case Well thank you for scrolling Bye for now. Until next time. Be well. FP edit: cheers everyone, ty and good night or good morning wherever thou are : )

https://twitter.com/NEJM/status/1509282168278687747?t=QkbVssWGUnGbjQCygrVjXA&s=19
Full study: https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMoa2115869
item.dc:creator: IbringthePANDAmonium

item.title: Pure evil: 193 House Republicans Voted Against Lowering The Price Of Insulin To $35
item.description: Who the fuck votes against this life saving treatment? Per AP: The Affordable Insulin Now Act will serve as a political vehicle to rally Democrats and force Republicans who oppose it into uncomfortable votes ahead of the midterms. For the legislation to pass Congress, 10 Republican senators would have to vote in favor. Democrats acknowledge they don’t have an answer for how that’s going to happen. “If 10 Republicans stand between the American people being able to get access to affordable insulin, that’s a good question for 10 Republicans to answer,” said Rep. Dan Kildee, D-Mich., a cosponsor of the House bill. “Republicans get diabetes, too. Republicans die from diabetes.” https://apnews.com/article/business-health-prices-medicare-inflation-c08ab8f2f58051f89d98346d53241a24
item.dc:creator: doxydad1
